vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1269

plan.title: feat(ritual): session sentinel + version-aware welcome-back banner for returning users

narratives.Description: feat(ritual): session sentinel + version-aware welcome-back banner for returning users
narratives.Overview:
## Why

The 4-step session-start ritual (#1149) is intentionally minimal and deterministic. Two failure modes for returning users are not currently covered:

1. **Recently-returning user (hours to ~1 week)** — gets the `task triage:summary` one-liner but no pointer back to their own in-flight work or to what changed in the last 24h. Has to recall trigger phrases (`what's next`, `queue`, `continue X`) to rehydrate state.
2. **Long-absence user (multiple minor versions behind)** — has no signal that the framework has moved. Picks up the new ritual but is silently unaware of new skills (gh-arch, cost, release, ...), new rules (#810, #798, #884, #954, #975, #1149, #1152, #1242), and new gates. Discovers the gap only when something fails.

Both gaps share one piece of missing infrastructure: a **per-clone session sentinel** that records what the user last saw, so the ritual can compute a delta deterministically instead of asking the LLM to guess from cache headers (which the cache-as-authoritative rule explicitly forbids).

## What ships in v1

### 1. Session sentinel

A per-clone, gitignored JSON file written at ritual exit:

```
.deft/last-session.json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "deftVersion": "0.32.0",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-20T20:00:00Z",
  "lastActiveVbrief": "vbrief/active/2026-05-13-1125-followup-scope-complete-and-spec-render.vbrief.json",
  "lastBranch": "feat/foo-bar"
}
```

- Written by a hook at the end of the session-start ritual (or by a dedicated `task ritual:sentinel-write` invoked by the ritual)
- Read at the start of the next session-start ritual to compute the delta
- Failure mode: missing/corrupt sentinel → fail open, treat as fresh user, do not block the ritual
- Privacy: never committed; add `.deft/` to `.gitignore`; do not store anything beyond version + timestamp + path + branch

### 2. Welcome-back banner (version-aware)

A 5th conditional line in the session-start ritual, fired only when the sentinel exists and the deft version has moved:

- `sentinel.version < HEAD.version` (any minor delta) →
  `[deft] Welcome back -- directive moved v0.28 -> v0.32 since your last session. See CHANGELOG.md for details.` (Editorial note: the `task whats-new --since=<version>` digest verb is a non-goal of v1 per the section below; banner v1 points at `CHANGELOG.md` until the digest ships in a follow-up; revised per SLizard advisory on PR #1271.)
- `sentinel.version == HEAD.version` → silent (no banner; ritual stays at 4 steps)
- `sentinel.version > HEAD.version` (shouldn't happen, but fail open) → silent

The banner is **a pointer**, not a digest. v1 ships the banner referencing `task whats-new --since=<version>`; the digest verb itself is a follow-up issue. The banner is still useful before the digest exists because it gives the user the signal that the framework moved.

## Non-goals for v1

- The `task whats-new --since=<version>` digest verb (separate follow-up; depends on #1242 CHANGELOG-entry-style maturity)
- In-flight resume nudge / `task vbrief:resume <path>` verb (separate follow-up; closes the symmetric resume-rehydration gap)
- 24h activity delta line for Cohort 1 (follow-up; needs sentinel as dependency)
- Stale-vBRIEF migration nudge for Cohort 2-deep (follow-up; needs sentinel as dependency)
- Auto-chain into `skills/deft-directive-sync/SKILL.md` for >90d absences (follow-up)

These all build on the sentinel. v1 lands the sentinel + the banner so downstream verbs become natural follow-ups.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `.deft/` added to `.gitignore`
- [ ] Sentinel reader + writer module (e.g. `scripts/ritual_sentinel.py`) with `read()` / `write()` / `compute_delta()` functions
- [ ] Sentinel writer invoked at end of session-start ritual (hook into the existing ritual orchestration; if no hook exists, add one)
- [ ] Welcome-back banner emission gated on `sentinel.version < HEAD.version`; silent otherwise
- [ ] Failure modes: missing sentinel, corrupt JSON, schema-version mismatch all fail open (no banner, no exception bubble-up)
- [ ] HEAD version source matches `Taskfile.yml VERSION` (or whatever the canonical version constant is); document the source
- [ ] AGENTS.md `## Session-start ritual (#1149)` updated to mention the 5th conditional step + cross-link this issue
- [ ] Unit tests covering: fresh-clone (no sentinel), same-version session, minor-bump session, major-bump session, corrupt sentinel, missing version field
- [ ] `task check` passes
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry under `[Unreleased]` in #1242-compliant style (lead with user-visible benefit, 2-4 sentences)

## Risks

- **Sentinel corruption** — must fail open under all conditions; never let a bad sentinel block the ritual
- **Version-source drift** — if `Taskfile.yml VERSION` and the version embedded in the sentinel disagree on shape, the version-compare must degrade gracefully (treat unparseable as "unknown" → silent)
- **Banner fatigue** — banner fires once per version-bump, not per session; document this so users don't expect a recap on every restart
- **Privacy** — sentinel is per-clone and gitignored; do not add identifying data beyond version + timestamp + path + branch

## Refs

- AGENTS.md `## Session-start ritual (#1149)` — the 4-step deterministic ritual this 5th conditional step extends
- AGENTS.md `## Cache-as-authoritative work selection (#1149)` — explains why this gap exists today (cache-driven, no memory-based recommendations)
- #1242 — CHANGELOG entry style; gates the future `task whats-new` digest verb that this banner points at
- Follow-ups (to be filed separately): `task vbrief:resume <path>`, `task whats-new --since=<version>`, 24h activity delta, stale-vBRIEF migration nudge

items.title: Sentinel reader + writer module (e.g. `scripts/ritual_sentinel.py`) with `read()` / `write()` / `compute_delta()` functions
Sentinel writer invoked at end of session-start ritual (hook into the existing ritual orchestration; if no hook exists, add one)

Welcome-back banner emission gated on `sentinel.version < HEAD.version`; v1 banner wording points at `CHANGELOG.md` (NOT the not-yet-existing `task whats-new` verb); silent when versions match (revised per SLizard P2 advisory on PR #1271)
Failure modes: missing sentinel, corrupt JSON, schema-version mismatch all fail open (no banner, no exception bubble-up)
HEAD version source matches `Taskfile.yml VERSION` (or whatever the canonical version constant is); document the source
AGENTS.md `## Session-start ritual (#1149)` updated to mention the 5th conditional step + cross-link this issue
Unit tests covering: fresh-clone (no sentinel), same-version session, minor-bump session, major-bump session, corrupt sentinel, missing version field
CHANGELOG entry under `[Unreleased]` in #1242-compliant style (lead with user-visible benefit, 2-4 sentences)
